question: Will Petr Yan fight Alexandre Pantoja next?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Petr Yan is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Petr Yan is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Yan is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Merab Dvalishvili (89.5%) as Petr Yan's next opponent, driven by reports of UFC targeting a bantamweight title rematch after Dvalishvili's unanimous decision win over Yan at UFC 288 and his recent vacant title capture from Sean O'Malley at UFC 306. Yan's ranking climb to No. 4 via decision victory over Tony Gravely in March bolsters his contention status, while limited elite options exist amid Umar Nurmagomedov's injury recovery and Deiveson Figueiredo's transition from flyweight. This implied probability reflects booking momentum for a high-stakes grudge match, though shifts could arise if Nurmagomedov heals quickly for a title queue jump or O'Malley pushes for immediate redemption, altering Yan's path.

question: Israel strike on Damascus by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Damascus Governorate, or any target within it, between December 8, 2:20 PM ET, and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact within Damascus Governorate.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land in Damascus Governorate, or cause damage.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting and video evidence. If there is ambiguity over whether a strike occurred, or whether the drone/missile was intercepted, this market will resolve based on whatever is most likely according to a consensus of credible reporting and video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Israeli airstrikes on Damascus international airport and Syrian military sites, including strikes on October 25 and November 3 targeting Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) assets, have intensified trader consensus on escalation risks in Syria. These operations align with Israel's longstanding policy of disrupting Tehran-backed networks amid the Israel-Hezbollah conflict spilling over from Gaza. Key drivers include confirmed IRGC reinforcements near Damascus and Syrian air defenses' limited effectiveness, as evidenced by official IDF statements. While diplomatic channels via the US and Qatar urge restraint, historical strike frequency—over 20 this year—bolsters yes-side probabilities. Watch for Hezbollah retaliation or UN talks this week, which could sway odds.

question: 9.0 or above earthquake before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if 1 or more earthquakes with a magnitude of 9.0 or higher occur anywhere on Earth between December 8, 2025 12:00 PM ET, and December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used. 

After a qualifying earthquake is registered, this market will remain open for 24 hours to account for any revisions to its recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: The market's 89% implied probability on "No" for a magnitude 9.0+ earthquake before 2027 stems primarily from the extreme rarity of such megathrust events, with USGS records showing only five verified occurrences worldwide since 1900—averaging one every 25 years. The last, Japan's 2011 Tohoku rupture at 9.1, remains distant, and current seismic monitoring via global networks detects no acute strain buildup or foreshock sequences priming any major subduction zone like Cascadia or Nankai Trough for imminent failure. Recent developments, including the January 2024 Japan 7.6 quake and ongoing Pacific Ring of Fire activity, fall far short of M9 thresholds, reinforcing trader consensus that short-term global odds hover below 10% based on historical baselines and probabilistic models.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader conviction in a 94.3% "No" probability for a magnitude 10.0 or greater earthquake before 2027 stems from the geophysical implausibility of such an event, as no quake exceeding 9.5—the 1960 Chile event—has ever been recorded by global seismic networks. USGS assessments highlight that a M10.0 would demand a fault rupture over 1,000 km long with energy release 5.6 times greater than a M9.5, exceeding known plate boundaries' capacities like the subduction zones in the Pacific Ring of Fire. Continuous monitoring by USGS and international agencies shows no precursors for such extremes, with historical baselines confirming events above M9.1 occur roughly once per decade at most. Realistic challenges include an unforeseen mega-thrust rupture in under-monitored areas like the Aleutians, though models cap maximums below 10.0 due to Earth's crustal limits.

question: Will Lando Norris be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: George Russell's commanding lead in the 2025 F1 Drivers' Championship standings, bolstered by Mercedes' dominant pace in recent races like Austin and Mexico City where he secured back-to-back victories, anchors his 55.5% trader consensus as title favorite with six rounds remaining. Kimi Antonelli's breakout rookie campaign, including multiple podiums and just 28 points adrift, fuels his 18.6% share amid Mercedes' teammate synergy and reliability edge over rivals. Charles Leclerc's 6.6% reflects Ferrari's inconsistent upgrades, while Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari transition struggles drop him to 5.5%; Max Verstappen's Red Bull reliability woes and 4.5% odds underscore McLaren challengers' fading momentum despite Lando Norris' flashes. Traders weigh sprint formats, tire degradation, and weather variables in the run to Abu Dhabi.

eventMetadata.context_description: George Russell's commanding lead in the 2025 F1 Drivers' Championship standings, bolstered by Mercedes' dominant pace in recent races like Austin and Mexico City where he secured back-to-back victories, anchors his 55.5% trader consensus as title favorite with six rounds remaining. Traders weigh sprint formats, tire degradation, and weather variables in the run to Abu Dhabi.

question: Will Flavio Bolsonaro qualify for Brazil's presidential runoff?
description:
General elections are scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026. If no candidate wins outright in the first round, the top two candidates proceed to a runoff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed candidate advances to Brazil's presidential runoff election or otherwise wins outright in the first round. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the president is decided in the first round of the election or the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for Brazil's 2026 presidential first-round advancers centers on President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, who lead recent nationwide polls from Datafolha and Quaest at around 28-32% and 25-29% respectively, far short of the 50% needed to avoid a runoff. No other candidate exceeds 8%, reinforcing expectations of a Lula-right-wing matchup. Key drivers include Lula's steady approval amid economic recovery efforts, contrasted by Tarcísio's rising momentum as a Bolsonaro successor proxy following strong state governance metrics. October 2024 municipal elections offer early indicators of voter shifts, while party conventions through 2025 will finalize slates amid potential legal challenges to eligibility. Odds capture polling volatility and historical base rates where polls have tightened late.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' commanding 78.5% implied probability in the 2025 F1 Constructors' Championship reflects their late-2024 surge, with multiple podium sweeps and the fastest car in recent races like Brazil and Abu Dhabi, bolstered by a stable lineup of George Russell and rookie Kimi Antonelli. Ferrari's 13.5% share stems from Lewis Hamilton's high-profile switch pairing with Charles Leclerc, potentially unlocking upgrades despite integration risks. McLaren (3.2%), the 2024 defending champions with Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri, faces pressure to sustain momentum amid tighter competition, while Red Bull (2.1%) contends with Honda engine transition uncertainties. Minimal odds for others like Williams or Alpine highlight power unit reliability gaps and underdeveloped chassis ahead of 2026 regulations.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 F1 Constructors’ Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No.”

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from F1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' commanding 78.5% implied probability in the 2025 F1 Constructors' Championship reflects their late-2024 surge, with multiple podium sweeps and the fastest car in recent races like Brazil and Abu Dhabi, bolstered by a stable lineup of George Russell and rookie Kimi Antonelli. Ferrari's 13.5% share stems from Lewis Hamilton's high-profile switch pairing with Charles Leclerc, potentially unlocking upgrades despite integration risks. McLaren (3.2%), the 2024 defending champions with Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri, faces pressure to sustain momentum amid tighter competition, while Red Bull (2.1%) contends with Honda engine transition uncertainties. Minimal odds for others like Williams or Alpine highlight power unit reliability gaps and underdeveloped chassis ahead of 2026 regulations.

question: Israel x Hamas Ceasefire Phase II by March 31?
description:
On October 8, an agreement on an initial ceasefire (Phase 1) was announced, constituting a halt in fighting, a partial Israeli pullback to a “yellow line,” prisoner swaps, expanded aid, and the release of hostages. A second phase of the deal, yet to be negotiated, is expected to address further Israeli withdrawal, Hamas’s disarmament, and the establishment of new security and governance arrangements in Gaza.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if an official agreement for the second phase of the Israel–Hamas ceasefire, defined as a publicly announced mutual agreement, is reached between Israel and Hamas by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

For purposes of this market, a “second phase” ceasefire agreement refers to a deal publicly acknowledged by both Israel and Hamas (directly or via recognized mediators such as the U.S., Egypt, or Qatar) that goes beyond the Phase 1 initial truce and includes commitments towards a comprehensive or “full” ceasefire.  Any agreement that is publicly described by the parties, or by mediators on their behalf, as the completion of “Phase 2” of the ceasefire will qualify, regardless of whether it includes further Israeli withdrawals beyond the “yellow line,” or agreements by Hamas to disarm.

An extension or continuation of the Phase 1 arrangement alone will not qualify.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Israel and Hamas, however a wide consensus of credible media reporting will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on an Israel-Hamas ceasefire Phase II leans skeptical, with low implied probabilities reflecting stalled indirect talks mediated by Qatar, Egypt, and the US. Hamas recently rejected a US-backed proposal demanding full Israeli withdrawal from Gaza and permanent end to hostilities, while Israel insists on sustained military pressure to dismantle Hamas leadership and secure remaining hostages. Recent escalations, including Israeli strikes killing senior militants and aid convoy incidents, have hardened positions amid over 40,000 reported Gaza deaths. Upcoming Doha negotiations and UN Security Council debates could shift dynamics, but historical negotiation impasses suggest persistent uncertainty for traders.

question: Will Albania qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This is a market on which teams will qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to qualify for the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g. they cannot reach the required number of points to advance from its group or qualify for playoffs), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or the qualifying stage has not been completed by September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be FIFA.  (https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup).

events.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Which countries qualify?
events.description: This is a market on which teams will qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

eventMetadata.context_description: USA, Canada, and Mexico hold automatic qualification as 2026 FIFA World Cup co-hosts, anchoring trader consensus while 45 spots remain contested in confederation qualifiers. Recent November internationals shifted dynamics: Argentina's 1-0 win over Brazil extended their CONMEBOL lead to 28 points, virtually securing a top-six berth alongside Brazil (24 points), while Venezuela's surge to 16 keeps them in playoff contention. In AFC's third round, Japan and Iran maintain dominance ahead of June finales. CAF groups stay chaotic with Nigeria and Egypt faltering. Upcoming UEFA league phase in March 2025, AFC/CAF conclusions, and intercontinental playoffs introduce volatility, as markets price form, remaining fixtures, and FIFA rankings.

question: Will the 10-year treasury yield hit 4.4% by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Treasury 10-year yield reaches or is higher than the listed value for any date between December 9, 2025 and March 31, 2026. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is the Department of the Treasury, specially the data listed under "Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates" for the column "10 Yr" (see: https://home.treasury.gov/resource-center/data-chart-center/interest-rates/TextView?type=daily_treasury_yield_curve&field_tdr_date_value=2025).

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders on Polymarket are pricing in a peak 10-year Treasury yield around 4.5% by March 31, driven primarily by persistent inflation pressures and robust U.S. economic data that have tempered expectations for aggressive Federal Reserve rate cuts. Current yield stands at 4.35%, up from 4.0% in late 2024, reflecting market-implied odds of just two 25bps cuts in 2025 per Fed funds futures. Key supports include sticky core PCE at 2.7% and strong payrolls, while fiscal deficit expansion adds upward pressure. Watch February CPI release on March 12 and the March 19 FOMC meeting for catalysts; yields above 4.75% would signal heightened stagflation risks, though recession fears cap aggressive rallies.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 70% implied probability to the 10-year Treasury yield peaking above 4% before 2027, a surge fueled by Friday's yield spike to 4.26%—highest since May—after a blockbuster 254,000 nonfarm payrolls report curbed expectations for deep Fed rate cuts. With yields closing near 4.15% amid resilient growth, sticky core PCE inflation at 2.7%, and ballooning fiscal deficits boosting supply pressures, market consensus tilts toward a higher peak versus September's Fed dot plot median of 3.4% terminal rate. Traders eye October CPI release on the 10th and November FOMC for shifts in cut pricing, though recession odds introduce downside uncertainty.

question: Will ACA premium tax credits not be extended and will the Democratic Party win the House in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the combined outcome of whether enhanced ACA premium tax credits will be extended in 2025 (https://polymarket.com/event/enhanced-aca-premium-tax-credits-extended-in-2025?) and according to which party will win the House in 2026 (https://polymarket.com/event/which-party-will-win-the-house-in-2026?).

The rules and resolution criteria are as follows:

1. Enhanced ACA premium tax credits extended in 2025?
Affordable Care Act (ACA) enhanced premium tax credits are set to expire at the end of 2025 if not extended by the federal government.

This market will resolve according to whether a bill extending the enhanced ACA premium tax credits beyond 2025 is signed into federal law by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. 

A qualifying bill may extend the enhanced ACA premium tax credits in any form, including shorter extensions, phased-down benefits, or narrowed eligibility, as long as the bill clearly continues enhanced premium tax credits that have wider eligibility and/or lower required income contributions relative to baseline ACA premium tax credits that would otherwise apply after 2025.

A bill replacing the ACA enhanced premium tax credits with an alternative form of healthcare subsidy, such as direct cash payments to a health savings account, will not qualify.

If the bill is signed into law before 2026, it will qualify to resolve this market, regardless of when it takes effect.

The primary source of resolution will be official information from the US federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

2. Which party will win the House in 2026?
This market will resolve according to the party that controls the House of Representatives following the 2026 U.S. House elections scheduled for November 3, 2026.

House control is defined as having more than half of the voting members of the U.S. House of Representatives.

If the outcome of this election is ambiguous given the above rules, this market will remain open until the Speaker of the House is selected following the 2026 U.S. general election, at which point it will resolve to the party the Speaker is affiliated with at the time of their election to that position. If the elected Speaker does not caucus with any listed party this market will resolve “Other”.

Determination of which party controls the House after the 2026 U.S. House elections will be based on a consensus of credible reporting, or if there is ambiguity, final federal and/or state election authority certification or other final official determination of the 2026 election results.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors ACA enhanced premium tax credits expiring after 2025 without extension, paired with Democrats regaining House control in 2026 at 81.5% implied probability, versus Republicans holding at 18.5%. This reflects Republican unified opposition under current White House and congressional majorities, as seen in the House GOP's recent budget resolution advancing cuts to the Biden-era subsidies costing over $300 billion through 2031. Extension appears improbable absent major flips, given fiscal hawkishness and Trump's pledges for ACA reforms without added spending. Democrats' edge for 2026 stems from the president's party historical midterm seat losses averaging 26, GOP's razor-thin 220-215 majority needing just three flips, and early generic ballot leads showing Dem +2-4 points amid special election gains. Upcoming debt ceiling and budget fights in early 2025 could further clarify trajectories.

question: Will there be another US government shutdown by January 31 and will the Democratic Party win the House in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the combined outcome of whether there will be another US government shutdown by January 31 (https://polymarket.com/event/will-there-be-another-us-government-shutdown-by-january-31?) and according to which party will win the House in 2026 (https://polymarket.com/event/which-party-will-win-the-house-in-2026?).

The rules and resolution criteria are as follows:

1. Will there be another US government shutdown by January 31?
This market will resolve according to whether the U.S. Office of Personnel Management (OPM) announces another federal government shutdown due to a lapse in appropriations by January 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Partial shutdowns count as shutdowns; announcements of office closures due to holidays or inclement weather do not qualify as a shutdown.

The resolution source for this market will be OPM’s Operating Status page (https://www.opm.gov/policy-data-oversight/snow-dismissal-procedures/current-status/).

2. Which party will win the House in 2026?
This market will resolve according to the party that controls the House of Representatives following the 2026 U.S. House elections scheduled for November 3, 2026.

House control is defined as having more than half of the voting members of the U.S. House of Representatives.

If the outcome of this election is ambiguous given the above rules, this market will remain open until the Speaker of the House is selected following the 2026 U.S. general election, at which point it will resolve to the party the Speaker is affiliated with at the time of their election to that position. If the elected Speaker does not caucus with any listed party this market will resolve “Other”.

Determination of which party controls the House after the 2026 U.S. House elections will be based on a consensus of credible reporting, or if there is ambiguity, final federal and/or state election authority certification or other final official determination of the 2026 election results.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a US government shutdown as nearly certain (98%) paired with Democrats retaking House control in 2026 at 83% odds, driven by Republicans' slim 220-215 majority vulnerable to internal fractures over spending and debt ceiling deadlines in January and March 2025. Recent near-miss in December 2024, when President-elect Trump and Elon Musk publicly derailed a massive continuing resolution amid conservative backlash against omnibus funding, averted closure but highlighted persistent partisan gridlock risks. Early generic ballot polls favor Democrats by 3-5 points, aligning with midterm history where the president's party loses an average 26 House seats, bolstering Dem recapture probabilities.

question: Will MrBeast hit 480 Million subscribers by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the MrBeast YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast) hits the specified number of subscribers by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be the MrBeast YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast) or a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will MrBeast hit ___ Million subscribers by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the MrBeast YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast) hits the specified number of subscribers by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be the MrBeast YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast) or a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: MrBeast's relentless upload cadence and viral philanthropy challenges have propelled trader optimism, with Yes shares trading at implied 72% odds for hitting 350 million YouTube subscribers by March 31. Sitting at 319.2 million as of mid-December, he needs roughly 30.8 million more in 15 weeks—a feasible 2.05 million weekly pace matching his post-"Squid Game" average of 1.8-2.5 million. Recent catalysts include a 250-million-view Thanksgiving special and Beast Games reality show buzz, amplifying algorithmic boosts. Holiday viewership spikes and teased 2025 mega-projects could accelerate gains, though algorithm tweaks or content flops pose risks; watch weekly sub tallies for position adjustments.

question: Taylor Swift x Travis Kelce get married by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift's marriage takes place by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM. Otherwise, this market will resole to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Taylor Swift x Travis Kelce get married by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's marriage takes place by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resole to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tying the knot remains cautious, with low implied probabilities reflecting the absence of any confirmed engagement amid rampant tabloid speculation. Their high-profile romance, ignited in summer 2023 with Swift's Eras Tour appearances at Chiefs games, has fueled "Swelce" mania—bolstered by joint holidays, Super Bowl celebrations, and recent cozy outings like a Sydney Zoo visit during her Australian tour leg. However, no verified reports of proposals or rings have emerged from credible sources, tempering optimism despite cultural hype around power couple dynamics. Key watchpoints include Swift's tour wrap in December 2024 and NFL playoffs, where personal milestones could shift odds, though secret celebrity nuptials often defy predictions.

question: Will Eddie Andrews win the 2026 Iowa Governor Republican primary election?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Iowa, scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Iowa Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Iowa Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Iowa Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Iowa, scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

eventMetadata.context_description: Randy Feenstra leads trader consensus for the Iowa Republican gubernatorial primary due to his incumbency as U.S. Representative for Iowa's 4th District, robust fundraising exceeding $1 million in recent filings, and established name recognition among conservative voters. Adam Steen trails as a Cedar Rapids businessman leveraging grassroots support and outsider appeal, while Zach Lahn draws from prior state House service but lags in resources. A June Emerson College poll showed Feenstra at 48%, Steen 23%, and Lahn 17%, mirroring market odds. Recent quarterly disclosures solidified Feenstra's financial edge, with Governor Kim Reynolds' anticipated endorsement as a pivotal upcoming factor amid stable field dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rob Sand's 97% trader consensus in the Iowa Democratic gubernatorial primary market reflects his dominant polling leads, robust fundraising exceeding $1 million early, and widespread name recognition from his tenure as State Auditor exposing government waste. Recent catalysts include his February 2025 campaign launch, key endorsements from figures like U.S. Rep. eventMetadata.context_description: Jeff Colyer leads trader consensus at 47% implied probability in the Kansas Republican gubernatorial primary market, bolstered by his experience as former governor and lieutenant governor, giving him strong name recognition among GOP voters. Senate President Ty Masterson trails at 34%, gaining traction from legislative leadership and endorsements within the state party establishment. Longshot candidates like Philip Sarnecki (12%) draw from grassroots conservative activism, while others including Charlotte O’Hara and Stacy Rogers lag due to limited visibility. Recent candidate announcements and early fundraising reports have solidified Colyer and Masterson as frontrunners, though no polls have emerged yet; upcoming donor disclosures and potential endorsements could shift odds ahead of the August 2026 primary.

This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Kansas, scheduled to take place on August 4, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Kansas Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Kansas Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Kansas Governor Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Kansas, scheduled to take place on August 4, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Ethan Corson at 74% implied probability to win the Kansas Democratic gubernatorial primary, driven by his strong fundraising lead—over $500,000 raised—and recent internal polling showing him ahead by double digits among likely voters. State Senator Cindy Holscher trails at 23%, bolstered by her legislative experience and endorsements from party insiders, though her lower cash reserves limit visibility. Marty Tuley lingers at 1.9% amid minimal campaign traction. A fresh Emerson poll released last week widened Corson's edge to 42%-28%, shifting odds upward, while the August 4, 2026, primary looms as the next catalyst for volatility.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hannah Pingree leads trader consensus at 31% implied probability in Maine's Democratic gubernatorial primary market, driven by her top fundraising haul exceeding $500,000 and legislative track record, yet Nirav Shah (23.5%) and Troy Jackson (19.5%) keep the field tight with Shah's pandemic-era popularity as ex-CDC director and Jackson's labor union backing as Senate president. Recent Emerson polling shows all three under 25% support, reflecting voter indecision without Gov. Janet Mills' endorsement in this open race. Clustered odds persist amid fragmented progressive and moderate lanes; catalysts for separation include Mills' nod, high-profile debates, or fresh surveys before the June 2026 primary.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Robert Charles dominates trader consensus at 64.5%** for Maine's Republican gubernatorial primary, propelled by his standout fundraising—recent FEC reports show him raising over $300,000, dwarfing competitors—and endorsements from influential state Republicans, including former officials. His Navy veteran and prosecutor background resonates with the GOP base seeking a strong challenger to Democratic incumbent Janet Mills in 2026. Ben Midgley (14%) and Jonathan Bush (10.9%) trail with modest grassroots momentum and local name recognition, while David Jones (10%) gains from business ties. Absent recent polls, markets hinge on early momentum and filings; no major catalysts have shifted odds lately, though candidate forums loom.

question: U.S. x Russia Nuclear deal by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if an official agreement on nuclear arms control or strategic nuclear weapons limitation is reached between the United States and the Russian Federation between December 9, 2025 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only agreements that are publicly announced and acknowledged by both the U.S. and Russia will qualify. The agreement must pertain to nuclear arms control or limitation—such as a treaty, framework, or memorandum—addressing matters including (but not limited to) nuclear warheads, delivery systems, verification mechanisms, or strategic stability.

An extension, amendment, or replacement of the New START treaty will qualify, provided it is publicly announced as a concluded agreement by both parties within the time frame. Exploratory discussions or stated intent to extend without a finalized agreement will not qualify.

Agreements that include both the United States and Russia as named parties—whether bilateral or as part of a broader multilateral framework—will qualify, provided the agreement directly addresses nuclear arms control.

The agreement does not need to be ratified or implemented for this market to resolve "Yes"; a formal announcement of a concluded agreement is sufficient.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government (e.g., the White House, State Department) and/or the Russian Federation (e.g., the Kremlin, Ministry of Foreign Affairs). However, a clear and overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming that such an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

Agreements that include both the United States and Russia as named parties—whether bilateral or as part of a broader multilateral framework—will qualify, provided the agreement directly addresses nuclear arms control.

The agreement does not need to be ratified or implemented for this market to resolve "Yes"; a formal announcement of a concluded agreement is sufficient.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government (e.g., the White House, State Department) and/or the Russian Federation (e.g., the Kremlin, Ministry of Foreign Affairs). However, a clear and overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming that such an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Russia-Ukraine war continues as the primary barrier to a U.S.-Russia nuclear arms control deal, with Moscow's 2023 suspension of New START inspections and demands for conflict resolution halting bilateral talks, per official State Department and Kremlin statements. No verified negotiations have advanced since President Biden's last Putin call in 2022, amid mutual accusations of treaty violations. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities, tempered by U.S. election rhetoric promising swift diplomacy under a potential Trump administration. Upcoming catalysts include the November 5 presidential vote and any post-election outreach, though historical arms talks like INF averaged years-long timelines amid similar tensions.

question: Will Aaron Rodgers retire before next season?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Aaron Rodgers retires from the NFL before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official announcement from Rodgers that he is retiring will qualify even if his official retirement has not yet occurred, as long as the announced retirement is stated to take effect before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Announcements that Rodgers will retire after the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Aaron Rodgers, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aaron Rodgers' public affirmations of continuing his career, coupled with his three-year contract extension through 2025 that includes significant guarantees, anchor the 77% implied probability traders assign to him not retiring before next season. Recent wins like the 28-27 thriller over Indianapolis signal sustained arm talent, outweighing age-related whispers or team struggles; historical precedents from peers like Tom Brady reinforce trader consensus that Rodgers eyes at least one more year barring unforeseen health setbacks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stacy Garrity's commanding 92.5% implied probability in the Pennsylvania Republican gubernatorial primary stems from her March 2025 campaign launch as the first major declared candidate, leveraging her incumbency as state treasurer and early fundraising momentum exceeding $1 million. eventMetadata.context_description: Jermaine Johnson holds a commanding 77% implied probability in the South Carolina Democratic gubernatorial primary market, driven by his early frontrunner status as a state representative with superior fundraising—over $500,000 raised by Q3 2024—and leading position in recent Winthrop University and internal polls showing him at 40-50% support among likely Democratic voters. Recent catalysts include Johnson's September endorsement from progressive groups like Planned Parenthood and a crowded field splintering anti-Johnson votes, with no major challengers like Joe Cunningham gaining traction; traders await Q4 finance reports and holiday polling for shifts ahead of the June 2026 primary.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pamela Evette leads trader consensus at 49.5% in the South Carolina Republican gubernatorial primary market, driven by her position as lieutenant governor, proximity to term-limited Governor Henry McMaster, and top fundraising totals exceeding $1.2 million through Q3 2024, per recent filings. Alan Wilson trails closely at 39%, bolstered by his decade as attorney general, strong conservative credentials, and nearly $1 million raised, positioning both as frontrunners in early Winthrop polling where Evette holds 24-28% support among GOP voters. Nancy Mace's 18.5% reflects her congressional name recognition despite polarizing national profile, while Ralph Norman and Josh Kimbrell lag amid lower polls and funds; recent quarterly disclosures reinforce the Evette-Wilson duel ahead of 2026 primary catalysts like further endorsements and surveys.

question: Will the Fed decrease interest rates by 50+ bps after the June 2026 meeting?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's June 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for June 16-17, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Federal Reserve rate change at the June 11-12 FOMC meeting, with 83.5% implied probability, driven by resilient economic data tempering cut expectations. May's nonfarm payrolls surged 272,000—well above 185,000 forecasts—while April CPI printed at 3.4% year-over-year, hotter than anticipated, signaling sticky inflation amid a 4% unemployment rate. Fed Chair Powell's recent hawkish remarks underscore a data-dependent pause at the current 5.25-5.50% fed funds range. Modest 10.5% odds for a 25 bps cut reflect lingering disinflation hopes, but upside risks like 5% for a hike remain tail events absent overheating. Upcoming ISM services data could sway final positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. Rep. Dusty Johnson's slight edge in trader consensus for the South Dakota Republican gubernatorial primary stems from his congressional name recognition and fundraising strength, yet rancher Toby Doeden's rapid rise to near-parity reflects his appeal to grassroots conservatives via self-funding and anti-establishment messaging. Lt. Gov. Larry Rhoden trails with executive experience but lacks momentum. The race remains tight amid sparse independent polling and an open seat left by term-limited Gov. This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Wisconsin, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Wisconsin Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Wisconsin Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives Mandela Barnes a slight edge at 40.5% implied probability to win the Wisconsin Democratic gubernatorial primary, with Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez close behind at 31%, reflecting a crowded early field lacking definitive polls. Barnes benefits from statewide name recognition as former lieutenant governor and 2022 Senate nominee, while Rodriguez leverages her current office and progressive credentials; Rep. Francesca Hong draws left-wing support at 17.8%. The race stays tight due to fragmented endorsements, modest fundraising so far, and no incumbent Tony Evers, who has not ruled out re-election. Separation could emerge from Q4 2024 finance reports, Evers' decision timeline, or initial public surveys anticipated in early 2025.

description:

events.title: Michigan Governor Democratic Primary Winner

eventMetadata.context_description: Jocelyn Benson's 91.5% implied probability in the Michigan Democratic gubernatorial primary reflects her commanding lead, fueled by superior fundraising—over $2 million raised early—key endorsements from EMILy's List and labor unions, and high name recognition as the incumbent Secretary of State with a record defending election integrity. Recent FEC filings underscore her financial edge, while rivals like Marni Sawicki, Chris Swanson, and Garlin Gilchrist trail in resources and polls showing Benson at 40-50% support. Trader consensus anticipates no major disruptions before the August 2026 primary, though realistic challenges include a high-profile entrant like a U.S. House member or unexpected polling shifts from upcoming candidate forums and state conventions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tom Tiffany commands 82% trader consensus as the Wisconsin Republican gubernatorial primary frontrunner, bolstered by his incumbency as U.S. Representative for the 7th District, proven fundraising strength exceeding $1 million early, and alignment with Trump-era conservatism that resonates in rural strongholds. Recent developments include Tiffany's October 2024 exploratory committee launch signaling firm intent for the 2026 race, alongside polling leads in early surveys from conservative outlets. Challengers like Tommy Thompson (3%) face age and retirement speculation at 82, while Rebecca Kleefisch (0.9%) and Sean Duffy (0.9%) linger on past 2022 primary losses and national media profiles without fresh momentum, keeping the August 2026 primary odds stable amid quiet field dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Wyoming Gov. Mark Gordon's July 16 suspension of his Republican primary reelection bid has propelled State Superintendent Megan Degenfelder to frontrunner status, with traders assigning her a 75.5% implied probability of winning the nomination. This shift reflects consolidation of conservative support behind Degenfelder, reinforced by recent polls showing her at 36% versus State Sen. Eric Barlow's 20% and Brent Bien's lower share, positioning Barlow at 15.8% and Bien at 4.2% in market odds. Gordon lingers at 2.2% despite his exit announcement citing family priorities amid intraparty attacks, while the August 20 primary remains the key upcoming event for further shifts in GOP gubernatorial sentiment.

This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Wyoming, scheduled to take place on August 18, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Wyoming Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Wyoming Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Wyoming Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Wyoming, scheduled to take place on August 18, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Wyoming Gov. Mark Gordon's July 16 suspension of his Republican primary reelection bid has propelled State Superintendent Megan Degenfelder to frontrunner status, with traders assigning her a 75.5% implied probability of winning the nomination. This shift reflects consolidation of conservative support behind Degenfelder, reinforced by recent polls showing her at 36% versus State Sen. Eric Barlow's 20% and Brent Bien's lower share, positioning Barlow at 15.8% and Bien at 4.2% in market odds. Gordon lingers at 2.2% despite his exit announcement citing family priorities amid intraparty attacks, while the August 20 primary remains the key upcoming event for further shifts in GOP gubernatorial sentiment.
